Fixed PowerStat script arguments by appending -R -z -d 0 1 3600 to prevent crashing when device is plugged into AC power (not discharging battery).
Test run data (21 runs) was from validation phase only. Real experiment runs not yet started.

GCP Carbon Footprint API has a 1-2 month reporting lag. fetch_gcp_energy.py must be run weeks after experiment runs, not immediately. run_experiment.py handles this via separate run_mode and fetch_mode.

Client-side Dual Measurement: Captured on the local Ubuntu host concurrently with edge and cloud workloads using measure_client_energy.py to compute symmetric comparisons immediately after runs.
Cloud Client Energy Fix: Resolved 0J issue in cloud runs by adding surgical sudo to measure_client_energy.py and fixing FIFO cross-user signaling. Verified with session cloud_verify_002.

Client measurement migrated to dedicated Machine B (192.168.1.105) on 2026-03-17. All client energy readings now come from an independent physical machine, eliminating measurement taint from edge server overhead. Edge and cloud client measurements are now fully symmetric.

Cloud workload traffic now originates from Machine B directly (iperf3 client, HTTP requests). Machine A handles topology, server-side Scaphandre/PowerStat, GCP metric polling, and database insertion only. FIFO-based measure_client_energy.py subprocess removed from both run_workload.sh and run_cloud_workload.sh.

client_daemon.sh on Machine B accepts start/stop/baseline commands. stop command prints JSON summary to stdout; Machine A bash scripts parse and insert into client_energy_runs. DB never touched from Machine B.

video_encoding and db_query workloads still execute on GCP server via SSH from Machine A. Machine B measures its own energy during the wait — records idle/overhead energy for those workload types. Noted in notes field of client_energy_runs rows.

Edge traffic now originates from Machine B over local network (192.168.1.x). topology.py server_only mode used — no dept containers. iperf3/HTTP servers run on Machine A host (not in container) because Containernet overrides Docker network namespace, preventing port_bindings from routing external traffic. Machine B sends iperf3/HTTP to Machine A's local IP on those ports. Client energy now symmetric between edge and cloud runs.
